effect: Disarms the target, forcing whatever they are holding (typically a wand) to fly out of their hand. Harry's signature spell.

firstAppearance.scene: Snape disarms Lockhart at the Duelling Club demonstration

effect: Conjures a corporeal animal Patronus that wards off Dementors and Lethifolds. Requires the caster to focus on a single intensely happy memory.

firstAppearance.scene: Lupin teaches Harry the Patronus Charm in private lessons using a boggart-Dementor in the History of Magic classroom

effect: Instantly kills the target with a flash of green light. One of the three Unforgivable Curses; has no known counter-curse.

firstAppearance.scene: Barty Crouch Jr. (disguised as Mad-Eye Moody) demonstrates the Unforgivable Curses on a spider in DADA class

effect: Inflicts unbearable, excruciating pain on the victim. One of the three Unforgivable Curses; prolonged use can drive the victim insane.

firstAppearance.scene: Barty Crouch Jr. (as Mad-Eye Moody) demonstrates the Cruciatus Curse on a spider in DADA class

effect: Places the victim under the caster's complete control, forcing them to obey any command. One of the three Unforgivable Curses; can be resisted only by exceptional willpower.

firstAppearance.scene: Barty Crouch Jr. (as Mad-Eye Moody) demonstrates the Imperius Curse by making a spider dance in DADA class

effect: Causes a target object to levitate into the air. The first formal spell taught to Hogwarts first-years.

firstAppearance.scene: Professor Flitwick's first-year Charms class, where Hermione corrects Ron's pronunciation ('it's leviOsa, not levioSA')

effect: Ignites a bright light at the tip of the caster's wand, allowing them to see in the dark.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry and Ron use it to navigate the spider-filled Forbidden Forest while following Aragog's trail

effect: Extinguishes the light produced by Lumos at the caster's wand tip. Counter-charm to the Wand-Lighting Charm.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry and Hermione extinguish their wands at the base of the Whomping Willow before following Sirius and Ron into the Shrieking Shack tunnel

effect: Summons an object to the caster, even from a great distance and out of sight. The caster must clearly visualize what they want.

firstAppearance.scene: Molly Weasley summons Fred and George's hidden Ton-Tongue Toffees at the Burrow before the Quidditch World Cup

effect: Unlocks doors, gates and other sealed objects. Ineffective against locks reinforced by stronger magic.

firstAppearance.scene: Hermione unlocks the door to the forbidden third-floor corridor while hiding from Filch, leading the trio to discover Fluffy

effect: Fires a jet of red light that stuns and knocks the target unconscious. A staple of duelling and Auror combat.

firstAppearance.scene: Ministry officials including Barty Crouch Sr. fire Stunners into the woods to apprehend whoever conjured the Dark Mark at the Quidditch World Cup

effect: Forces a Boggart to assume a humorous form chosen by the caster, since Boggarts are defeated by laughter.

firstAppearance.scene: Lupin's third-year Defence Against the Dark Arts class with the staffroom-wardrobe boggart, where Neville faces Snape first

effect: Slashes the target as if with an invisible sword, causing deep, bleeding wounds. Invented by the Half-Blood Prince and labelled 'For Enemies'.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry casts it on Draco in the sixth-floor boys' bathroom after Draco tries to use Crucio on him; Snape arrives to heal Draco

effect: Erases specific memories from the victim's mind. Used by the Ministry to hide magic from Muggles; powerful enough to cause permanent amnesia if cast strongly.

firstAppearance.scene: Lockhart tries to wipe Harry and Ron's memories in the Chamber of Secrets tunnel with Ron's broken wand, and the spell backfires onto himself

effect: Seamlessly mends broken or torn objects, restoring them to their original condition.

firstAppearance.scene: Arthur Weasley uses it to repair Harry's broken glasses at the Leaky Cauldron after Harry's exit from Knockturn Alley

effect: Conjures a jet of clean, drinkable water from the tip of the caster's wand.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry refills the crystal goblet for Dumbledore in the seaside cave guarding Slytherin's locket Horcrux

effect: Causes the target to explode in a burst of flame. A combat-grade blasting curse popular in duelling.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry casts it from the falling motorbike sidecar during the Battle of the Seven Potters while fleeing Privet Drive

effect: Cuts, tears or severs objects with surgical precision.

firstAppearance.scene: Ron uses the Severing Charm on the lace cuffs and ruff of his Yule Ball dress robes in an attempt to make them less embarrassing

effect: Causes the target object to swell to many times its normal size. Counter-charm: Reducio.

firstAppearance.scene: Barty Crouch Jr. (as Mad-Eye Moody) enlarges the three spiders before demonstrating the Unforgivable Curses on them

effect: Shrinks an enlarged object back to its normal size. Counter-charm to the Engorgement Charm.

firstAppearance.scene: Barty Crouch Jr. (as Mad-Eye Moody) uses it to shrink the demonstration spiders back down after the Unforgivable Curses lesson

effect: Heals relatively minor injuries such as broken noses, split lips and broken toes.

firstAppearance.scene: Tonks fixes Harry's broken nose on the Hogwarts Express after Draco stomps on it under the Invisibility Cloak

effect: Conjures bandages and a splint around a wounded limb, easing pain and immobilising broken bones.

firstAppearance.scene: Lupin splints Ron's broken leg in the Shrieking Shack after Sirius's dog-form drags him through the Whomping Willow tunnel

effect: Terminates the effects of currently active spells in its area. A general counter-spell.

firstAppearance.scene: Snape ends the chaos at the Duelling Club, cancelling out the Tickling Charm, Tarantallegra and other spells flying between paired students

effect: Causes the victim's skin to erupt in painful boils.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry casts it at Draco in a corridor confrontation after the Quidditch World Cup; the spell collides with Draco's Densaugeo and hits Goyle instead, covering his face in boils

effect: Creates an exact duplicate of the target object. The curse form causes touched items to multiply uncontrollably and burn the toucher.

firstAppearance.scene: Hermione duplicates Slytherin's locket after stealing the real one from Umbridge during the trio's infiltration of the Ministry of Magic

effect: Slows or temporarily freezes the target's movement, useful for halting an attacker or fleeing creature.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry uses it inside the Triwizard maze to slow down a Blast-Ended Skrewt and later an Acromantula during the Third Task

effect: Conjures thick magical ropes that fly out of thin air and bind the target.

firstAppearance.scene: Umbridge binds the centaur Magorian after he threatens her in the Forbidden Forest, just before the herd attacks her

effect: Conjures a jet of flames that can set objects alight or fill a fireplace.

firstAppearance.scene: Arthur Weasley lights a fire in the Dursleys' boarded-up fireplace at Privet Drive when collecting Harry for the Quidditch World Cup

effect: Hoists the victim into the air by the ankle, leaving them dangling upside-down. Invented by the young Severus Snape.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry reads the spell in the margins of the Half-Blood Prince's potions book and accidentally casts it nonverbally on Ron in their dormitory

effect: Conjures the Dark Mark, a colossal skull-and-serpent image, in the night sky. Used by Death Eaters as a calling card after a killing.

firstAppearance.scene: Barty Crouch Jr. fires the Dark Mark into the sky using Harry's stolen wand after the 1994 Quidditch World Cup, terrifying the Death Eaters who had abandoned Voldemort

effect: Fills the ears of anyone nearby with an unidentifiable buzzing, allowing the caster to hold a private conversation in public. Invented by the Half-Blood Prince.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry finds the spell scribbled in the margin of his second-hand Advanced Potion-Making and the trio quickly adopt it for private conversations

effect: Locks the victim's body completely rigid; arms snap to sides, legs spring together, and they topple over. Eyes remain mobile.

firstAppearance.scene: Hermione stuns Neville with it in the Gryffindor common room when he tries to stop the trio from leaving to find the Philosopher's Stone

effect: Conjures an invisible magical shield that deflects spells and physical attacks back at the attacker.

firstAppearance.scene: Hermione teaches it to Harry while drilling him for the Third Task of the Triwizard Tournament, with Ron firing hexes for him to deflect

effect: Sends a jet of silver light into the target that triggers uncontrollable laughter and tickling, weakening them. Often used as a diversion in duels.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry casts it on Draco at Lockhart's Duelling Club; it is the first incantation Harry has ever spoken aloud with a wand in canon

effect: A cleaning charm that scrubs the target object clean, often producing soap bubbles.

firstAppearance.scene: Tonks cleans out Hedwig's filthy cage when the Advance Guard collects Harry from Privet Drive to take him to Grimmauld Place

effect: Renders the victim temporarily mute. Especially useful against opponents who must vocalise their incantations.

firstAppearance.scene: Flitwick teaches fifth-years the Silencing Charm using bullfrogs and ravens; Hermione masters it instantly while Harry and Ron struggle

effect: Forces the victim's legs into uncontrollable, frantic dancing, leaving them unable to stand still or aim properly.

firstAppearance.scene: Draco casts it on Harry during their Duelling Club match, sending Harry's legs into a manic jig before Snape ends the duel

effect: Blasts solid objects apart in a burst of force. Useful for breaking through barriers but can be lethal against living targets.

firstAppearance.scene: Harry blasts through the hedges of the Triwizard maze during the Third Task while practising for and navigating the labyrinth

effect: Suspends an unconscious body a few inches off the ground and moves it forward, like a magical stretcher.

firstAppearance.scene: Lupin levitates the Stunned Snape back through the Whomping Willow tunnel toward Hogwarts after the Shrieking Shack confrontation

effect: Conjures a live snake from the caster's wand tip.

firstAppearance.scene: Draco produces a black serpent against Harry at Lockhart's Duelling Club, prompting Harry to accidentally reveal his Parseltongue ability

effect: Locks the victim's legs together, preventing them from walking or running.

firstAppearance.scene: Draco hits Neville with the Leg-Locker Curse in a Hogwarts corridor; Neville hops back into the Gryffindor common room and Hermione undoes it

effect: Directs conjured creatures or animated objects under the caster's control to attack the target.

firstAppearance.scene: Hermione sets a flock of conjured canaries on Ron after he walks into the room with Lavender Brown, hand in hand
